title: Chinese Embassy condemns US arms package to Taiwan as violation of one-China principle
summary: The Chinese Embassy in the United States issued a statement condemning the latest US arms sale notification to Taiwan, calling it a grave violation of the one-China principle and three joint communiques.
contents:
# Chinese Embassy Condemns Latest US Arms Sale to Taiwan

## Official Statement
The Embassy of the People's Republic of China in Washington released a statement on May 1, 2026, strongly condemning the US State Department's notification to Congress of a new arms sale package for Taiwan.

## Key Points from Statement
- Characterizes the sale as a "grave violation" of the one-China principle
- References the three China-US joint communiques as the foundation of bilateral relations
- Warns of "resolute countermeasures" if the sale proceeds
- Demands the US "immediately revoke this wrong decision"
- States the sale "seriously damages China's sovereignty and security interests"

## Arms Package Details
The notified package reportedly includes advanced air defense components and maritime surveillance systems, though specific quantities remain subject to Congressional review.

## Diplomatic Context
The statement follows a pattern of Chinese diplomatic protests accompanying each US arms notification to Taiwan. Beijing views such sales as interference in its internal affairs and a threat to regional stability.

## Tags
